ops.title: Fleet gotcha: RLIMIT_NPROC ulimit is `-u` on bash/macOS but `-p` on dash — bites any #!/bin/sh script
ops.body:
When centralizing a shell script as a cc-guides artifact, cc-guides sh-lint forces `#!/bin/sh`. On Ubuntu (incl. GitHub Actions `ubuntu-latest`), `/bin/sh` is **dash**, and dash spells the per-process RLIMIT_NPROC limit as `ulimit -p` — while **bash and macOS `/bin/sh` spell it `ulimit -u`** (in bash, `-p` is the pipe-buffer size, a completely different limit). Verified on-box: `dash -c 'ulimit -Hu'` → "Illegal option -u"; `dash -c 'ulimit -Hp'` works and genuinely caps nproc.

Consequence: a naive `#!/bin/sh` + `ulimit -u` under dash ERRORS, and with `set -eu` it aborts the whole script — silently removing a fork-bomb guard on CI. The fleet's `scripts/test.sh` fork-bomb guard hit exactly this when it moved from `#!/usr/bin/env bash` (always bash → `-u` fine) to the cc-guides-mandated `#!/bin/sh`.

Portable idiom (shipped 2026-07-19 in the shared cc-skills fragment `plugin/guides/sh/go-test-harness.sh`):

```sh
hard="$(ulimit -Hu 2>/dev/null || ulimit -Hp 2>/dev/null || echo unlimited)"
ulimit -Su "$cap" 2>/dev/null || ulimit -Sp "$cap" 2>/dev/null ||
  echo "scripts/test.sh: warning: shell exposes no RLIMIT_NPROC control; fork-bomb cap NOT applied" >&2
```

bash/macOS-sh take `-u`; Ubuntu dash takes `-p`; an exotic shell with neither warns and continues (best-effort, matching the script's cur/hard-limit ethos). Verified 48/48 identical computed caps across bash, /bin/sh, and dash over a matrix of ps-counts × headrooms. Any future `#!/bin/sh` centralization of a ulimit-bearing script must use this two-spelling form, never bare `-u`. See [[cc-guides-shell-artifact-adoption-recipe-test-sh-gen-version-xcconfig-sh-with-posix-gotcha]].
